experience.title: Social Media And Community Executive
experience.description: I worked as a Social Media Executive at WeSwap, a unique peer-to-peer travel money platform, helping travellers everywhere get the fairest exchange rates. My role was to manage and maintain WeSwap's Facebook, Instagram, Twitter and LinkedIn accounts. My responsibilities included: - Developing and executing a social media strategy for each platform - Creating weekly reports to update the marketing team and business as a whole on engagement, following and social trends - Writing copy for all posts, ensuring they meet the brand tone of voice - Working closely with the design team to brief in and produce on-brand imagery to be used across all platforms - Develop and manage competitions, giveaways and campaigns to increase following and engagement - Run paid social campaigns for Facebook and Twitter - Managing public and private messages on all review platforms including Trustpilot, CompareHolidayMoney, Google Reviews and App Store reviews Achievements included: - Executing largest competition to date across WeSwap's social channels - Creating and implementing a process and training for customer support agents to reply to private messages on social platforms - Working with the CRM manager to create a social specific email as part of the customer journey - Producing videos as part of paid social campaigns - Forming relationships with a variety of small scale influencers to represent and advertise the brand

experience.title: Social Media Executive
experience.description: I worked as a Social Media Executive for a luxury travel tech scale-up that is in the process of building the world’s most rewarding travel & lifestyle platform. I managed social media accounts for clients in the UK, US and Canada, including the Montcalm and Park Grand Hotel chain. Responsibilities included: - Creating and implementing monthly social strategies for over eight accounts. - Ensuring all posts are scheduled and delivered on time to the correct platforms - Communicating social media trends and industry insights to clients - Managing and responding to public and private comments across all accounts - Providing monthly reports with updates on engagement, follower increase and global social trends

linkedin_profile.headline: EVP | Commercial Director at Edwardian Hotels London - The Londoner, The May Fair, The Edwardian Manchester
linkedin_profile.about: Hasnain is proud to serve as the Commercial Director of Edwardian Hotels London, one of the largest privately-owned hospitality companies in the UK. A highly experienced hotelier, he has successfully worked across a range of roles within the sector during a career spanning more than two decades. An exemplary project manager, with a proven track-record of managing large teams, he started his current role in 2016 and is responsible for developing and implementing commercial strategies to accelerate growth and revenue across the group. He is devoted to understanding the expectations of guests to ensure that their needs are met at all times and inspires his diverse group of teams to create and provide exceptional experiences for anyone who walks through their hotel doors. Part of this work includes collaborating with all disciplines including the hospitality group’s in-house tech team to develop innovative services to improve customer experience. As a leader in the business, Hasnain has been instrumental in planning for The Londoner, the latest project by Edwardian Hotels London, set to open its doors late 2020 as the world’s first Super Boutique hotel in London’s Leicester Square. Hasnain is an avid user of social media, recognising its benefits of engaging with customers one-on-one as well as at scale. He also finds it benefits his understanding of key trends and traveller behaviours. In his personal time, Hasnain is committed to volunteering at Age UK.

experience.title: Group Digital Marketing Manager
experience.description: As the Group Digital Marketing Manager at Apex Hotels, I lead a dynamic team of three specialists to drive our digital presence across multiple channels. Our mission is to enhance brand visibility, optimise customer engagement, and maximise conversion rates to support the company’s growth objectives. Key Responsibilities: - Strategic Leadership: Spearhead the planning, execution, and optimisation of digital marketing strategies across Paid Search, Paid Social, Display, Email, SEO, Affiliate, and Metasearch channels. - Team Management: Manage and mentor a team of three: a PPC & SEO Specialist, a Paid Media Specialist, and a Digital Marketing Executive, fostering a collaborative and innovative work environment. - Performance Analytics: Develop measurement strategies and KPIs to assess campaign performance, leveraging tools like Google Analytics and Google Tag Manager for in-depth analysis and insights. - Campaign Optimisation: Plan and refine digital campaigns, ensuring alignment with business objectives and budget constraints to achieve maximum ROI. - Market Research: Identify new market opportunities by researching trends and conducting keyword and audience analysis, ensuring our strategies are ahead of the curve. - Stakeholder Collaboration: Partner with key stakeholders in the wider commercial team to maximise opportunities, aligning digital strategies with broader business goals. Achievements: - Successfully increased direct revenue and website traffic across various business sources, including Accommodation, Food & Beverage, Meetings & Events, and Spa. - Enhanced the performance and workflows of the in-house digital marketing team, significantly increasing efficiency and campaign effectiveness. - Developed and implemented a comprehensive digital strategy that supports city centre and country house hotels, restaurants, meeting spaces, and spas, ensuring tailored approaches for diverse market segments.

# Maritime Aquarium Welcomes New Harbor Seal In Norwalk

## The Maritime Aquarium in Norwalk has announced the arrival of a new 8‑year‑old harbor seal at the facility.

###### RJ Scofield, Patch Staff

Posted Sun, Apr 26, 2026 at 12:01 pm ET

The Maritime Aquarium in Norwalk announced the arrival of Kaya, a new 8‑year‑old harbor seal, on April 20, 2026. (RJ Scofield/Patch Staff)

NORWALK, CT — The Maritime Aquarium announced this week the arrival of a new 8‑year‑old harbor seal at the Norwalk facility.

According to a news release provided by the aquarium, Kaya arrived in Norwalk earlier this month from the Aquarium of the Pacific in California and is now on exhibit at the Maritime Aquarium's Pinniped Cove exhibit.

linkedin_profile.about: Michelle Edwards is the Director of Public Relations and Communications at Four Seasons Resort Oahu at Ko Olina. A 13-year luxury hospitality veteran, Michelle brings a wealth of public relations expertise, luxury brand know-how and innovation to the project. She is responsible for the development and implementation of the resort’s overall strategic public relations initiatives. Michelle joins Four Seasons Resort Oahu at Ko Olina most recently from Mauna Lani, Auberge Resorts Collection as the Director of Public Relations and was part of the opening resort team. Prior to Mauna Lani, she served as a strategic communications consultant for various Four Seasons properties globally, including Four Seasons Resort Oahu, Four Seasons Hotel Kuala Lumpur, Four Seasons Resort Langkawi, Four Seasons Hotel Doha, Four Seasons Costa Rica, and Four Seasons Hotel Austin. Prior to her consultant role, Edwards was the Director of Public Relations at Four Seasons Resorts Lanai, overseeing public relations for both Manele Bay and The Lodge at Koele. Michelle, who has been at the publicity helm of a myriad of hotel and restaurant openings throughout her career, has garnered global recognition for her innovation, creative vision and flawless execution in developing unique storytelling campaigns and cultivating groundbreaking experiential activations.

Additionally, was asked to task force two years in a row, for seasonal opening of the Sunset Beach Hotel in Shelter Island as well as front office management task force at Standard, Miami Beach. Promoted from Assistant Front Desk Manager to Front Desk Manager within the first 6 months after opening after showing strong control of front office department needs and flexibility to the challenges of the operation while focusing on the guest and employee experience.

experience.title: Lead Coordinator - Centre Of Business Engagement
experience.description: The position entailed working with various Managers and Coordinators within the department to manage and coordinate Industry events and initiatives. Additionally, helped on the Media Team to coordinate live Television Debates during the WEF in Davos, Press Junkets and Photographer communication. I was responsible for about 18 Team Coordinators to support and ensure the accurate progress of the projects and events.

experience.title: Director Of Rooms - Sydell Group
experience.description: Joined the pre-opening team for the 168-room luxury boutique hotel as Front Office Manager recruiting and training all employees for concierge, pbx, room service and bell team. Promoted to Director of Rooms in June, 2013 and continued to oversee 80 employees across front office, guest services, housekeeping and engineering departments. Additional assignment: opening task force in various operational roles at Line Los Angeles (398 rooms).

experience.title: General Manager At Casa Faena
experience.description: A short-term assignment responsible for all construction renovation items which included a total structural and decorative renovation of the historic 50-room property. Once reopened, managed the re-branding and marketing to a Faena branded property. Hotel was repositioned and staff retrained resulting in higher guest and staff satisfaction. One example of the successful retraining was our Tripadvisor rating from #202 to #9 in all of Miami Beach.

experience.title: Director Of Operations
experience.description: Due to my relationship with the Ian Schrager team from past projects, I took on a short-term, 1–year assignment to assist in opening the 273-room Edition Clocktower hotel. Reporting directly into the GM, I was responsible for all union operating departments including 40 housekeeping attendants, 8 front office agents, 3 concierges, 10 bellmen, 8 pbx/guest service agents, 6 room service servers and a team of about 30 in the lobby bar including managers, bartenders, bar backs and servers.

experience.title: Director Of Rooms - The Ned
experience.description: Collaborated with hotel General Manager in opening of 300K square foot luxury hotel with 272-rooms, nine restaurants, a fitness/wellness center, and private members club in the city of London. Created pre-opening budgets for guest rooms & hotel-related operations. Created standard operating procedures (SOPs), recruited, trained and managed the rooms team, including 40 housekeeping attendants, 30 front of house team members and 8 guest service agents out of a total of 700+ employees.

experience.title: General Manager - Sydell Group
experience.description: Reporting into the COO and CEO directly, I managed one of New York City’s most successful boutique hotels with 168-luxury rooms, 140-seat Michelin-starred restaurant and the James Beard award winning NoMad bar. Oversaw all aspects of hotel, including finance, sales, human resources, engineering, revenue, guest experience, events, capital expenditure, and budgets. Collaborated with restaurant General Manager to direct food and beverage (F&B). Pre-pandemic, along with my team of department heads, I was responsible for 320 employees in total. Promoted from Hotel Manager to General Manager in 2018. Additional assignments included opening task force for sister properties: Ned London (272 rooms), Line Washington DC (220 rooms), Nomad Las Vegas (293 rooms), Freehand New York (400 rooms) and the upcoming Nomad London (93 rooms). In 2020, closed and reopened Nomad, NY and Line DC due to pandemic and was involved in all strategic planning to get the properties safely and successfully through the pandemic. Some of these strategies in 2020 and into 2021 involved creating new “business models” for the property such as pivoting all F&B outdoors and developing a robust delivery service. Managed re-closure of Nomad New York property due to corporate restructuring in April, 2021.

experience.title: Founder

# BEST CONFERENCE VENUE

#### 116 Pall Mall

116 Pall Mall is a Grade I listed Georgian building opened in 1828, serving as home to the Institute of Directors and one of London’s premier conference venues. Located on prestigious Pall Mall, it combines distinguished heritage with modern event capabilities delivered through warm Searcys hospitality.

Post-COVID transformation saw remarkable growth from 15 to 80 staff across seven departments, achieving 67% revenue increase from £4.5 million to £7.5 million. The venue accommodates 12 to 350 delegates, featuring the versatile Nash Room with stunning period décor and modern technical capabilities.

Our competitive advantage stems from central location, comprehensive service delivery, and holistic approach combining venue hire, catering, technology, and event management. Key innovations include hybrid event technology, ESG strategy with 20% waste reduction ta

About us — 116 Pall Mall

# About us

## Introducing 116 Pall Mall

A special event calls for an extraordinary venue.

A stunning example of Georgian architecture and elegance, 116 Pall Mall is a Grade I listed building presiding over one of London’s most prestigious addresses. Opened in 1828, it’s now home to the Institute of Directors.

116 Pall Mall also plays host to hundreds of corporate occasions, private events, festive celebrations and weddings every year, bringing a sense of occasion to even the most intimate gatherings. Guests are instantly captivated by the building’s luxurious interiors, complete with magnificent period features alongside its unique heritage and the impeccable service provided by our team.

We offer a wide range of versatile and flexible spaces throughout our five floors, welcoming up to 1,500 guests, and our enviably central location makes travel to your event a breeze.

experience.title: Supervisor CRM Specialist
experience.description: Started as Sales Consultant in 2015, promoted to Senior Sales in 2016, Trainee Supervisor in 2017 and further promoted to Supervisor by end of 2017. Key Achievements & Responsibilities: • In 2016 and 2017, contributed personal sales over £1M and was awarded the Dior Million Club. • Delivered a CRM action plan to senior management after completing the Dior Management Program. Commended by Management team for implementing innovative strategies to maximize CRM and sales. • Appointed to CRM Specialist; led all CRM activities and supported a team of 24 to meet all business objectives including CRM and Event KPI’s. • Worked alongside CRM and Marketing Manager to create local campaigns and events aimed at building and maintaining an active client database, and acquiring new prospects. • Reviewed feedback and conducted research on products and customer purchase behavior, increased sales by 11% due to the findings and made improvement on service and campaigns. • Increased data capture rate significantly by 12% in 2 months, by implementing incentives and designing training sessions and materials to support team on successful data capture.

experience.title: Assistant Department Manager
experience.description: Developed and led a sales team of 15, including 2 Supervisors and 3 CRM Specialists. Responsible for managing all aspects of the day-to-day operations within the department. This includes driving growth and retention through Sales, CRM, Marketing, Customer Service and Visual Merchandising. Key Achievements & Responsibilities: • Successfully restructured the department and stabilised the team as they were left without an AM for 6 months during which figures and team performance reduced significantly. • Finished the year with strong results, delivering a 17% uplift in sales vs. the same period LY through supportive leadership, team motivation and CRM strategies. • Designed and implemented a customer onboarding plan, using lead logs to monitor and convert prospects and leads. Successfully grew RTW’s client base by 21% in 2018. • Built a Pre-Launch strategy in store, specifically focusing on client experience, promoting new collections and driving deposits before the launch day. • Contributed to Dior’s New Saddle Bag Launch and Campaign - the biggest launch since store opening; executed animations and strategies that generated incredible store traffic with a 24% increase in sales.

experience.title: Retail Support Manager
experience.description: Responsible for managing the key aspects of the store's business, including Sales and Retail Marketing; covering CRM, product launches and in-house events. Key Achievements & Responsibilities: • Managed a team of 4; end-to-end planning and execution of local email campaigns, activations, and loyalty programs to increase retention, engagement and drive sales. • Oversaw a large database with 6 different segments - actively identified new segments to target and planned marketing activities accordingly. • Worked with Digital team to design and develop a CRM mobile application; implemented and streamlined functions, including dashboard, contact/outreach and data capture which improved the overall sales productivity and the data capture rate significantly from 80% to 94% in 3 months. • Effectively boosted sales by 15% and increased in-store appointments during a slow selling period through creating a store animation, visual concept and e-mail campaign. • Increased local engagement by redesigning the local activation plan (Gifting, Birthday and Welcome) and created guidelines and communication tools for store to implement. • Developed loyalty and engagement by creating exclusive and innovative in-house events, experience and animations. • Successfully planned and executed over 12 events with an average ROI of 25%, including Cruise 19 Trunk Show – over 300 clients invited, generated £240K on the day of the event and led the 3 months follow-up campaign which converted 35% into high-value sales. • Led Omni-Channel project end-to-end; created a Client Relations department that responded to digital inquiries and referred leads to sales teams, increasing conversion by 13%. Additionally reduced customer complaint by 11% by resolving all client issues.

linkedin_profile.headline: Commercial Vice President for Red Carnation Hotels
linkedin_profile.about:
With a robust leadership role at Red Carnation Hotels, my focus is on shaping global commercial strategies for an exquisite collection of luxury boutique hotels. The heart of my role lies in managing and strategically developing sales, marketing, revenue, distribution, and reservations teams. Success in these areas has been marked by the launch of four new properties, creating a central reservations office, leading significant technology projects such as Opera Cloud PMS, Salesforce CRM and a guest service app, whilst showcasing a commitment to sustainable luxury in the hospitality industry. Collaborating closely with strategic agency partnerships, we have optimized revenue growth opportunities across 18 properties worldwide. My approach is both creative and data-driven, ensuring that every decision aligns with our mission to deliver unparalleled customer experiences. This includes a keen emphasis on customer acquisition, conversion, retention, and cross/up-selling, all while fostering authentic, purpose-driven initiatives that support sustainable travel.

Thomas Kochs: The Visionary Luxury Hotel Leader Behind Claridge’s Global Prestige - News Dip

Thomas Kochs

Thomas Kochs is one of the most respected figures in the global luxury hospitality industry, known for his impeccable leadership, refined operational vision, and long-standing contribution to world-class hotel experiences. His name has become synonymous with elegance, precision, and the highest standards of service excellence. Rising from an apprenticeship in Germany to becoming Managing Director at some of London’s most iconic luxury hotels, Kochs’ journey reflects commitment, discipline, and passion for hospitality at its finest.

Today, Thomas Kochs serves as the Managing Director at Claridge’s, one of the most prestigious hotels in the world. His previous leadership roles at Corinthia London, Hotel Café Royal, and The Connaught further define his distinguished career.

# Thomas Kochs Appointed General Manager of Claridge’s in London

| Travel News Asia | Latest Travel News | Podcasts | Videos | Friday, 14 January 2011 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

Maybourne Hotel Group, the owner and operator of London’s renowned luxury hotels Claridge’s, The Connaught and The Berkeley, has appointed Thomas Kochs as General Manager of Claridge’s, the iconic Mayfair landmark.

Kochs, who has worked in all three Maybourne properties since joining the company in 2001, most recently held the position of Hotel Manager at Claridge’s. In this role he successfully oversaw projects such as the creation and launch of the Diane von Furstenberg rooms and suites as well as ending 2010 with the best financial results in the hotel’s recent history.

From 2007 to 2009, Kochs held the title of Hotel Manager at The Connaught and played a key role in spearheading the successful re-launch of the prestigious hotel after
